questions.question: What is a major advantage of a microkernel compared to a monolithic design?
answers: Lower context switching overhead costs
Higher system stability and isolation of corrupted drivers
Faster execution performance speeds
Smaller total storage size footprint

questions.question: What happens during a hardware interrupt?
answers: Virtual memory maps flush physical hard drive tracks
The operating system completely reboots its kernel
The active process is terminated and marked as a zombie
The CPU suspends its execution stream to run an interrupt service routine

questions.question: What privilege layer does the operating system kernel run in on modern CPUs?
answers: Hypervisor mode
Kernel mode (Ring 0)
Safe mode
User mode (Ring 3)

questions.question: What characterizes a monolithic kernel architecture?
answers: All operating system services run inside a single privileged kernel space
It eliminates context switching overhead entirely
OS services are moved to separate user-space modules
It runs exclusively on virtual machines

questions.question: What is a major advantage of a journaling file system like ext4?
answers: It records changes in a log to prevent corruption during sudden power losses
It compresses text files to half their initial disk sector allocations
It speeds up graphics card rendering rates
It encrypts all user credentials automatically

questions.question: What does an inode store in a Unix-like file system?
answers: The human-readable file name and directory path string
The literal plain-text contents of the target file
The encryption keys used for secure network transmissions
File metadata, size, permissions, and block pointers, but not the file name

questions.question: What is a major disadvantage of the Shortest Job First scheduling algorithm?
answers: High context-switching processing overhead
It requires specialized graphical hardware layers
Long-running processes can suffer from starvation
Massive memory cache utilization footprints

questions.question: What information is tracked inside a Process Control Block (PCB)?
answers: Network interface card MAC address parameters
Local database storage tables metadata
Process state, program counter, CPU registers, and memory boundaries
The user web browser cache profiles

questions.question: Which scheduling algorithm runs processes based on a fixed time-slice quantum rotation?
answers: Multilevel Feedback Queue
Shortest Job First (SJF)
Round Robin (RR)
First-Come, First-Served (FCFS)

questions.question: What state does a process enter if it is waiting for an I/O operation to finish?
answers: Ready state
Running state
Zombie state
Blocked / Waiting state
